attacks.vector: Attacker measures response time of POST /login to infer password length/prefix.
attacks.mitigation: Password compared with crypto.timingSafeEqual over equal-length buffers. On length mismatch a dummy timingSafeEqual(expected, expected) runs, then returns false, so wrong-length and wrong-content both consume comparison time.
attacks.evidence: Wrong password (any length) -> 303 /login?error=1 with no cookie; no early-exit branch on length.

attacks.vector: Attacker crafts sess=<token>.<sig> without knowing SESSION_SECRET.
attacks.mitigation: verifyToken recomputes HMAC-SHA256(secret, token) and timingSafeEqual-compares the signature. Length guard prevents Buffer length-mismatch throw.

attacks.vector: Attacker takes a valid cookie and mutates it.
attacks.mitigation: Any mutation to token or signature invalidates the HMAC.

attacks.vector: Operator forgets SESSION_SECRET in production; system silently allows access.
attacks.mitigation: SESSION_SECRET defaults to '' -> every verifyToken returns false -> all requests 302 /login (fail closed). Startup console.warn is emitted.
attacks.evidence: Production, no SESSION_SECRET: startup warning logged; / -> 302 /login; fabricated cookie -> 302 /login.

attacks.vector: Operator forgets DASHBOARD_PASSWORD; login accepts empty/any password.
attacks.mitigation: Login action throws 500 when DASHBOARD_PASSWORD or SESSION_SECRET is missing (fail closed); it never issues a cookie.
attacks.evidence: Code path: if (!password || !secret) throw error(500). Not exercised at runtime because the fail-closed gate already blocks unauthenticated access; verified by reading.

attacks.vector: Attacker hits /api/* hoping the SvelteKit proxy forwards to Django before auth runs.
attacks.mitigation: Auth gate runs FIRST in handle(), before the /api/ and /node-api/ proxy blocks.
attacks.evidence: Unauthenticated /api/stats/ -> 302 /login (Django not even contacted; Django was not running during the test).

attacks.vector: Auth gate + pre-existing tracker redirect create an infinite /login <-> /tracker loop, denying all access.
attacks.mitigation: Public paths (/login, /logout, /_app/, /favicon.ico) return resolve(event) immediately, before the tracker redirect.

openItems: nginx /api/ location routes directly to Django, bypassing this gate. Documented in docs/deployment/README.md (auth-gate bypass warning). Ops must remove or IP-restrict it — outside this plan's code scope.
